subsection.heading: Establishment.—
subsection.content: There is established a National Towing Safety Advisory Committee (in this section referred to as the “Committee”).

subsection.heading: Function.—
subsection.content: The Committee shall advise the Secretary on matters relating to shallow-draft inland navigation, coastal waterway navigation, and towing safety.

paragraph.heading: In general.—
paragraph.content: The Committee shall consist of 18 members appointed by the Secretary in accordance with this section and section 15109 of this chapter.

paragraph.heading: Expertise.—
paragraph.content: Each member of the Committee shall have particular expertise, knowledge, and experience in matters relating to the function of the Committee.

paragraph.heading: Representation.—
paragraph.chapeau: Members of the Committee shall be appointed as follows:

subparagraph.content: 7 members shall represent the barge and towing industry, reflecting a regional geographic balance.
1 member shall represent the offshore mineral and oil supply vessel industry.
1 member shall represent masters and pilots of towing vessels who hold active licenses and have experience on the Western Rivers and the Gulf Intracoastal Waterway.
1 member shall represent masters of towing vessels in offshore service who hold active licenses.
1 member shall represent masters of active ship-docking or harbor towing vessels.
1 member shall represent licensed and unlicensed towing vessel engineers with formal training and experience.
2 members shall represent port districts, authorities, or terminal operators.
2 members shall represent shippers and, of the 2, 1 shall be engaged in the shipment of oil or hazardous materials by barge.
2 members shall represent the general public.
